question: What is one of the primary reasons given for using Hadoop on large clusters?
options: Nodes fail every day and failure is expected.
The number of nodes in a cluster is constant.
It is inexpensive to build reliability into individual applications.
correct_answers: Nodes fail every day and failure is expected.

question: In what year did Hadoop become an Apache Top Level Project?
Which of the following companies are listed as users of Hadoop?
Which of the following use cases are identified for Hadoop?
What is the role of the NameNode in the HDFS Architecture?
options: It stores the actual data blocks.
It manages the file system namespace and metadata.
It acts as a load balancer for DataNodes.
correct_answers: It manages the file system namespace and metadata.

question: Which of the following are characteristics of the NameNode?
options: It maps a file name to a set of blocks.
It executes read/write requests from clients.
It maps a block to the DataNodes where it resides.
It stores metadata only in memory, not on disk.
correct_answers: It maps a file name to a set of blocks.
It maps a block to the DataNodes where it resides.

question: Match the HDFS component to its primary function regarding data.
options: Stores data in the local file system.

correct_answers.A: Stores data in the local file system.

question: What is the purpose of the block report sent by the DataNode?
options: To request new data blocks from the NameNode.
To report the health of the physical hard drive.
To list the blocks hosted on that specific DataNode.
To confirm the successful execution of a Map task.
correct_answers: To list the blocks hosted on that specific DataNode.

question: In the context of HDFS replica placement, where are the three replicas typically placed?
options: One on the client's node, two on random nodes in the cluster.
One on a node in the local rack, one on a different node in the local rack, one on a node in a different rack.
All three on the same node to ensure fast local access.
All three on different racks to maximize fault tolerance, ignoring network bandwidth.
correct_answers: One on a node in the local rack, one on a different node in the local rack, one on a node in a different rack.

question: When a client reads data from HDFS, from which DataNode does it typically fetch the data?
options: Always from the DataNode on the remote rack.
From a randomly selected DataNode containing a replica.

question: What is the primary function of the CheckpointNode?
options: To fetch FsImage and EditLog from the NameNode to create a new FsImage.
To act as an active backup for the NameNode in case of failure.
To store the actual data blocks and perform heartbeats.
correct_answers: To fetch FsImage and EditLog from the NameNode to create a new FsImage.

question: How does HDFS ensure data correctness?
options: By relying on the underlying hardware's ECC memory.
By using an end-to-end checksum calculated by the client.
By storing data redundantly in memory.
By running a continuous background verification task on the NameNode.
correct_answers: By using an end-to-end checksum calculated by the client.

question: Which of the following are components of the NameNode Metadata?
options: List of Blocks for each file
List of DataNodes for each block
correct_answers: List of Blocks for each file
List of DataNodes for each block

question: Which part of the NameNode metadata is NOT kept on disk?
options: List of Blocks for each file
List of DataNodes for each block
correct_answers: List of DataNodes for each block

question: Match the DataNode failure type to the corresponding NameNode action.
options: DataNode is marked as dead and its blocks are replicated.
NameNode instructs DataNode to delete the block.
NameNode initiates replication for the under-replicated block.

correct_answers.A: DataNode is marked as dead and its blocks are replicated.
correct_answers.B: NameNode instructs DataNode to delete the block.
correct_answers.C: NameNode initiates replication for the under-replicated block.

question: What is the purpose of the DataNode Cluster Rebalancer?
options: To balance the CPU load across DataNodes.
To automatically move data from heavily utilized DataNodes to lightly utilized ones.
To redistribute metadata across multiple NameNodes.
correct_answers: To automatically move data from heavily utilized DataNodes to lightly utilized ones.
